ops.text: Fix Gradle build failure by switching to Gradle Wrapper (./gradlew) to avoid Gradle 9 incompatibilities

ops.find_block:
- name: Build APK
        run: gradle assembleDebug
ops.replace_with:
- name: Build APK
        run: chmod +x gradlew && ./gradlew assembleDebug

operations.find_block:
- name: Build APK
        run: gradle assembleDebug
operations.replace_with:
- name: Build APK
        run: chmod +x gradlew && ./gradlew assembleDebug

ops.text: Decouple Idle and Message track memory (Track-Aware branching)

The following actions are running on Node.js 20 and may not work as expected: actions/checkout@v4, actions/download-artifact@v4, google-github-actions/auth@v2, google-github-actions/setup-gcloud@v2. Actions will be forced to run with Node.js 24 by default starting June 2nd, 2026. Node.js 20 will be removed from the runner on September 16th, 2026. Please check if updated versions of these actions are available that support Node.js 24. To opt into Node.js 24 now, set the FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true environment variable on the runner or in your workflow file. Once Node.js 24 becomes the default, you can temporarily opt out by setting ACTIONS_ALLOW_USE_UNSECURE_NODE_VERSION=true. For more information see: https://github.blog/changelog/2025-09-19-deprecation-of-node-20-on-github-actions-runners/

OUTPUT: specific error, file/line, brief summary.

data.analysis:
**Specific Error:** 
`ERROR: (gcloud.firebase.test.android.run) [artifact_drop/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk] not found or not accessible`

**File/Location:** 
The error occurs during the execution of the `gcloud firebase test android run` command (Line ~213).

**Brief Summary:**
The CI/CD pipeline failed because the `gcloud` CLI could not locate the APK file specified in the `--app` argument. While the artifact `myandroid-apk` was successfully downloaded to the `artifact_drop` directory, the path provided to the Firebase Test Lab command (`artifact_drop/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk`) does not exist within that directory structure. 

**Recommended Fix:**
Verify the internal structure of the downloaded `myandroid-apk.zip` file. You may need to adjust the path in your workflow file (e.g., check if the `app/build/...` folder structure is preserved or if the APK is located directly in the root of the extracted artifact). You can add a `ls -R artifact_drop` command before the Firebase step in your workflow to inspect the directory contents and confirm the correct path.

ops.text: Fix nullability compile error in CommandProcessor
